title: Amphilophium elongatum
taxonomy: Amphilophium elongatum is classified within the family Apocynaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This plant is further classified under the subfamily Apocynoideae, and its species is a member of the Apocynum genus. The Apocynaceae family is a large and diverse group, consisting of around 5,000 species. Within the Apocynoideae subfamily, Amphilophium elongatum is part of the Apocynum genus, along with several other species. The exact classification of Amphilophium elongatum may vary depending on the source and the current taxonomy.
